pattern: Keeping one sorted structure of available copies per movie and one global sorted structure of rented copies turns search, rent, drop, and report into ordered-set inserts, removals, and prefix reads.
hints: search needs the cheapest copies of one movie ordered by (price, shop); a per-movie sorted set of (price, shop) gives the top five directly.
report needs the cheapest rented copies overall ordered by (price, shop, movie); a single global sorted set of (price, shop, movie) gives that prefix.
rent moves an entry from its movie's available set into the global rented set, and drop moves it back; store each entry's price keyed by (shop, movie) so both moves are O(log n).

edge_inputs.description: statement example: search ordering, two rents, a report, a drop, then search again
single shop, single movie: search, rent, empty search, drop, search again
search tie-break on shop number when prices match
report tie-breaks by shop then movie when prices equal
search returns at most five even when more copies exist
report is empty when nothing is rented
rent then drop restores the copy to search ordering

stress.description: random catalog with interleaved search/rent/drop/report respecting that rent only targets available copies and drop only targets rented ones
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 20)
    num_movies = rng.randint(2, 8)
    seen = set()
    entries = []
    for _ in range(rng.randint(5, 30)):
        shop = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        mv = rng.randint(1, num_movies)
        if (shop, mv) in seen:
            continue
        seen.add((shop, mv))
        price = rng.randint(1, 50)
        entries.append([shop, mv, price])
    if not entries:
        entries.append([0, 1, 1])
        seen.add((0, 1))
    ops = ['MovieRentingSystem']
    args = [[n, [e[:] for e in entries]]]
    available = set(seen)
    rented = set()
    for _ in range(size):
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.35 and available:
            shop, mv = rng.choice(sorted(available))
            available.discard((shop, mv))
            rented.add((shop, mv))
            ops.append('rent')
            args.append([shop, mv])
        elif r < 0.55 and rented:
            shop, mv = rng.choice(sorted(rented))
            rented.discard((shop, mv))
            available.add((shop, mv))
            ops.append('drop')
            args.append([shop, mv])
        elif r < 0.8:
            ops.append('search')
            args.append([rng.randint(1, num_movies)])
        else:
            ops.append('report')
            args.append([])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: available/rented sets keep rent and drop calls valid per the problem guarantees

complexity.time: O(log n) per rent/drop, O(1) search/report prefix
